title: Stranded in Open Water (Life Raft)
symptoms: Shipwreck or plane crash at sea
immediate_steps: Deploy the sea anchor to stabilize the raft and stay near the crash site for rescuers
Rig a shade canopy to protect from intense sun
Keep the raft dry by bailing out water
Collect rainwater using the canopy or containers
Maintain a continuous watch for ships, aircraft, and land
avoid: DO NOT DRINK SEAWATER (it will kill you through kidney failure)
Do not eat fish or birds unless you have a steady supply of drinking water (digestion consumes water)

title: Floating without a Raft (Drownproofing)
immediate_steps: Take a deep breath and let your body hang limp vertically in the water
Allow your face to submerge while your lungs keep you buoyant
When you need to breathe, raise your arms, part your legs, and push down gently to lift your mouth above the surface
Exhale rapidly, inhale deeply, and return to the relaxed limp position
If wearing pants, tie the legs in a knot, sweep air into the waist, and use it as a makeshift float
avoid: Do not panic and thrash (you will sink and exhaust yourself)
Do not try to keep your head above water constantly if you are tired
